codeReview.summary: Core multi-select and bulk flows are largely implemented, but two correctness gaps remain: bulk Change Manager is not undoable as a single unit, and the manager picker allows creating invalid self/cyclic reporting relationships.

issues.description: The Change Manager path performs per-employee `moveEmployee` calls (lines 25-33) instead of a batch command. `moveEmployee` records individual `type: 'move'` undo entries (`packages/client/src/stores/orgStore.ts:177-195`), so one Cmd/Ctrl+Z only reverts the last employee instead of the whole bulk operation, violating the feature requirement that bulk operations undo/redo as a single unit.

Manager options are built from all active/planned employees and do not exclude selected employees. Combined with applying manager changes to every selected ID (`packages/client/src/components/bulk/BulkOperationsToolbar.tsx:29-33`), users can assign an employee as their own manager. The server move endpoint directly persists `managerId` without cycle validation (`packages/server/src/controllers/employeeController.ts:150-154`), which can violate the tree invariant (no cycles) documented in `.factory/library/architecture.md:58`.

`rangeSelect` falls back to selecting only the target when `lastClickedId` is missing. In list views, plain row clicks clear selection without setting an anchor (`packages/client/src/components/views/HierarchyView.tsx:417-419`, `packages/client/src/components/views/SpreadsheetView.tsx:223-227`), so Shift+Click after a normal click does not form a range. This is a UX mismatch with expected Shift+Click range behavior.

summary: Review failed due to two blocking issues: Change Manager bulk operations are not grouped into single-step undo/redo, and manager bulk updates can create invalid self/cyclic hierarchy links. Also noted a non-blocking Shift+Click anchor UX gap in list views.
